# orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_schema (Data Source)

Reads the ServiceNow field schema for a given credentials resource — the same list the Orca
UI shows when you build a SIR or ITSM template. Useful when authoring or auditing the
`mapping_json` argument on
[`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_template`](../resources/integration_servicenow_sir_template.md)
and
[`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template`](../resources/integration_servicenow_itsm_template.md).

SIR and ITSM share the same `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource` credentials, but
map to different ServiceNow tables (`sn_si_incident` vs `incident`) and therefore return
different field sets. Select the variant with the `type` argument.

Backs `GET /api/resources/{resource_id}/service_now/{type}/schema`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Read the list of fields a ServiceNow resource exposes for a given table variant. Useful for
# authoring or auditing the `mapping_json` argument on the SIR/ITSM template resources.
# SIR and ITSM share the same credentials resource but map to different tables, so set `type`
# to the variant you are configuring.

# Security Incident Response (sn_si_incident table)
data "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_schema" "sir" {
  resource_id = "a6188626-fd15-4c0d-a262-9f3deffb5459"
  type        = "sir"
}

# IT Service Management (incident table)
data "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_schema" "itsm" {
  resource_id = "a6188626-fd15-4c0d-a262-9f3deffb5459"
  type        = "itsm"
}

output "sir_elements" {
  value = data.orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_schema.sir.elements
}

output "itsm_elements" {
  value = data.orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_schema.itsm.elements
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `resource_id` — (Required, String) ID of the ServiceNow credentials resource (an
  `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource` resource ID).
* `type` — (Required, String) Which ServiceNow table schema to read: `sir` (Security Incident
  Response, `sn_si_incident`) or `itsm` (IT Service Management, `incident`).

## Attribute Reference

* `elements` — (List of String) Flat list of field element names. These are the
  keys you can place in `mapping_json`.
* `fields` — (List of Object) Full schema entries. Each item exposes:
    * `element` — Field element name (the key used in `mapping_json`).
    * `label` — Human-friendly label as shown in ServiceNow.
    * `type` — Field type (`string`, `integer`, `reference`, etc.).
    * `default_value` — Default value Orca will use if you don't supply a mapping.
    * `max_length` — Max length the field accepts.
    * `choice` — Choice-list indicator if the field is a reference/picklist.
